items.title: Enhance Claude Model Dropdown with Enum Entries in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update restores the model selector on Claude native document pages by adding explicit model enum entries in the OpenAPI schemas for two endpoints. The changes ensure that the dropdown functionality is available, allowing users to select from a comprehensive list of models, including newly added options like 'claude-fable-5' and 'claude-sonnet-5'. Validation checks confirm that the updates align with the expected model visibility.

items.title: Enhance CSV Export Stability in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update stabilizes the usage CSV export by addressing intermittent failures caused by HTTP protocol errors. The implementation now uses keyset-paginated batch reads instead of a cursor-based stream, ensuring a more reliable data flow. Additionally, error handling has been improved to log failures and provide a terminal CSV line instead of causing a hard connection reset.

items.title: Enhance AI Chat with Claude Fable 5 in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the newly launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Sonnet 5 to the AI chat skill examples. The changes ensure that our documentation reflects the latest capabilities following the live rollout of these models. The update includes a minor modification to the representative examples list in the SKILL.md file.

items.title: Streamline BananaRouter's Retry Logic in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the self-retry mechanism for the BananaRouter nano-banana worker, simplifying the error handling process. The retry loop has been replaced with a single axios call, ensuring that only one upstream attempt is made per channel attempt. A new Jest test has been added to confirm that transient 503 errors result in a single call without delays.

items.title: Enhance Video Production with Scenario Selector in Nexior 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a new Video Type selector in the Maestro component, allowing users to specify the production scenario for video requests. Supported scenarios include options like 'drama', 'explainer', and 'product', with 'auto' set as the default. Additionally, internationalization keys have been added across 18 locales to support this feature.

items.title: Enhance Maestro API with Scenario Parameter in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new `scenario` parameter to the Maestro Videos OpenAPI, allowing for more tailored video production types. The `scenario` enum includes options like `drama`, `general`, and `explainer`, while maintaining the default `auto` setting for existing users. This change is a schema-only addition, ensuring no impact on current functionality.

items.title: Enhance Failover Routing for Nano-Banana in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update finalizes the channel order for the nano-banana failover router, introducing a new third channel, `bananarouter`, and moving `openaihk` to last-resort status. Key improvements include enabling failover on header timeouts and reducing response header timeout from 1 hour to 5 minutes, enhancing reliability during outages. The changes have been live-verified and are expected to improve overall performance and resilience.

items.title: Enhance Video Workflow with Scenario Knob in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'scenario' knob to the maestro worker, allowing users to select specific video workflows instead of relying on auto-routing. Additionally, it implements audio and imagery gates to ensure that videos are delivered with sound and appropriate visuals, addressing issues identified in a recent live test. The changes enhance user control and improve video quality significantly.

items.title: Enhance CSV Export Performance with Composite Indexes in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces composite indexes on the ApiUsage table to resolve performance issues with CSV exports that were timing out. The changes include creating indexes for user_id and created_at, and optimizing the export view to reduce unnecessary scans. These improvements will significantly enhance the efficiency of data retrieval for usage reports.

items.title: New Dedicated NanoBanana Service for Enhanced Scalability in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a dedicated `platform-service-bananarouter-nanobanana` pod and service, allowing for independent scaling and failure management. The service split enhances clarity and performance, while disabling internal transient retries behind the failover-router improves response efficiency. The changes align with existing nano providers, ensuring consistent deployment practices.

items.title: Fix CORS Header Issues for Anthropic Models in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves intermittent 401 CORS errors when using Anthropic-backed chat models by stripping unnecessary browser headers before forwarding requests. Key changes include modifications to the header handling logic to prevent the `Origin` and other browser-specific headers from reaching the upstream, which enhances security and reduces latency. New tests have been added to ensure that the filtering logic works correctly and that no critical headers are inadvertently removed.

items.title: Enhance failover routing with openaihk as last-resort channel in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces `openaihk` as the third and last-resort channel in the nano-banana failover router, ensuring it only activates when both primary channels are down. The maximum attempts for failover have been increased from 2 to 3, allowing for better recovery during outages. This change aligns the live configuration with the intended setup and improves the overall resilience of the service.

items.title: Sync Claude CLI Model Enum with Latest Docs in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the Claude CLI model enumeration with the latest OpenAPI specifications. The new model `claude-fable-5` has been added to the CLI commands, enhancing the functionality of `claude chat`, `claude messages`, and `claude count-tokens`. Additionally, the command-output assertions have been updated to ensure the new model is included in the output.

items.title: Exciting Integration of Claude Fable 5 in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've successfully integrated Anthropic's new `claude-fable-5` into our Claude API service, offering it at a competitive 0.80x of the official pricing. Key updates include new pricing rules, visibility adjustments in the API, and comprehensive testing to ensure accuracy. This enhancement is set to improve our service offerings significantly.

items.title: Streamline Health Checks by Dropping Image Probes in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the image health-check probes from the upstream health-checker, significantly reducing runtime and costs associated with these checks. Image models will now auto-pass checks, maintaining their routing without impacting overall performance. The changes include the removal of unused helper functions and adjustments to the test suite to reflect these updates.

items.title: Restoring the iOS Brand Icon for Desktop in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the iOS brand icon source back to the original full AceData brand mark, correcting the previous change that used a cropped logo. The fix ensures that the icon appears correctly in the Dock and taskbar across macOS and Windows platforms. Key modifications include updates to the icon generation script and the restoration of the proper asset source.

items.title: Enhance API Docs with 'Copy for AI' Button in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: We've added a 'Copy for AI' button to the API documentation detail pages, allowing users to easily copy structured API information in Markdown format. This feature extracts key details such as API title, request endpoints, authentication requirements, and more, making it ideal for sharing with AI assistants. Key files modified include the API info component and a new utility for formatting API definitions.

items.title: Enhance CSV Export for Full Date Range in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue where CSV exports were limited to the newest 10,000 rows, ensuring users can now retrieve the full selected date range. Key improvements include removing the row cap, implementing streaming for large datasets, and adding a truncation warning if the export exceeds one million rows. Additionally, the export now aligns with the list view's perspective and enforces the necessary OAuth scope for security.

items.title: Streamline Failover Router by Removing OpenAIHK in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: The `openaihk` channel has been removed from both routes of the nano-banana failover-router ConfigMap, optimizing the routing process. This change eliminates unnecessary HTTP 500 errors and wasted attempts, ensuring smoother operation with the remaining channels, `cqtai` and `mountsea`. The update mirrors a live hotfix already applied to the HK cluster, maintaining consistency across deployments.

items.title: Display 'Processing' Tag for Async Tasks in Frontend Web ⚡
items.summary: This update introduces a new feature that displays a yellow 'Processing' tag in the status code column of the usage list when the status code is empty, indicating that an asynchronous task is still in progress. The change affects the List.vue component and updates multiple localization files to include the new tag in various languages. This enhancement improves user experience by reducing confusion over empty status codes.

items.title: Restore macOS Dock Icon Badge in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update restores the white rounded badge behind the glyph for the macOS Dock icon, addressing visibility issues on dark wallpapers. The `build_icns()` function has been modified to composite the glyph onto a white background, while the Windows icon remains unchanged with full transparency. Key files updated include `gen-brand-icons.py` and the Podfile for iOS dependencies.

items.title: Restoring Previous State for VS Code Copilot Chat in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the recent changes made for VS Code Copilot Chat compatibility, restoring the files to their state prior to the last two PRs. Key files, including `history.py` and `connection.py`, have been reverted to eliminate the non-functional integration. All tests have passed successfully, ensuring stability in the current implementation.

items.title: Enhance chat title reliability in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the chat title retrieval process by implementing a fallback mechanism to read the chat-pane header when the input Edit title is stale. It addresses issues where newly renamed groups displayed a generic placeholder instead of the correct name, ensuring that chat verification functions correctly. The changes include new unit tests and adjustments to the title retrieval logic for better accuracy.

items.title: Reverted output-side scrub for enhanced confidentiality in Wisdom Channel 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the output-side regex scrub to reinforce confidentiality through the system prompt instead. Key changes include the removal of the scrub module and related configurations, while strengthening the existing prompt guardrails to explicitly address sensitive information. The overall impact ensures that confidentiality is maintained without relying on a denylist filter.

items.title: Update Font Awesome Icons to Latest Version in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the @fortawesome/free-solid-svg-icons package from version 7.2.0 to 7.3.0. Key changes include improvements and new icons as per the latest release notes, enhancing the iconography available for the project.

items.title: Enhance Outbound Reply Security with Confidentiality Scrub in Wisdom Channel 🔒
items.summary: This update introduces a robust confidentiality scrub for all outbound WeChat replies, ensuring that sensitive upstream provider aliases and internal identifiers are redacted before messages are sent. The scrubber operates through a denylist stored in code and an optional configuration file, preventing any potential leaks. Key features include customizable redaction modes and comprehensive unit tests to validate functionality.

items.title: Enhance Copilot Chat Continuation for VS Code in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability to continue VS Code Copilot Chat sessions on mobile devices by seeding the transcript into a new session. Key changes include the implementation of a method to check if a session is natively resumable and a function to build a compact preamble for the transcript. This enhancement ensures a seamless user experience when transitioning between devices without losing context.

items.title: Enhance Copilot Session Sharing in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability for the agent to read VS Code Copilot Chat transcripts, integrating them into the coding-bridge history seamlessly. Users will see these sessions listed as standard Copilot sessions without any differentiation, enhancing the user experience. Key changes include modifications to session reading logic and the addition of new test cases to ensure functionality.

items.title: Enhance Android Consent Dialog to Match Desktop Experience in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a native 3-tier consent dialog for Android's Computer Use feature, aligning it with the desktop version. Users can now choose between 'Allow once', 'Always allow', and 'Deny', enhancing user control and experience. Key changes include the implementation of a new `confirmConsent` method and updates to localization files across 18 languages.

items.title: Enhance MCP Server Management in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a per-server connection status feature for the MCP, allowing users to see whether each server is connected, failed, or disabled. It also adds an enable toggle and a Test/Reconnect button for easier management. Additionally, cross-platform spawn issues have been addressed, ensuring that GUI-launched applications can properly locate necessary executables on macOS, Linux, and Windows.

items.title: Enhance Guest Browsing Experience in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the forced login prompt for native and desktop applications, allowing guests to browse without immediate authentication. The login process is now deferred until a user initiates an action, ensuring a smoother experience across all platforms. Key changes include modifications to the App.vue file and the introduction of a new patch for authentication handling.

items.title: Enhance Fish Service with Introduction Page in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive landing page for the Fish Audio TTS service, filling the previously empty primary tab. Key additions include detailed sections on features, pricing, and usage examples, all designed to align with existing service introduction pages. The changes also include a migration script to ensure the new content is properly rendered in the application.

items.title: Updated VS Code Badges for All Extensions in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the retired shields.io VS Code Marketplace badge across 20 README files for various extensions. The new static badge ensures that users see a valid link instead of a retired message, enhancing the user experience. The changes will take effect once the extensions are republished to the Marketplace.

items.title: Polish Taskbar Icon and Credits Pill Alignment in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update addresses two key issues: the taskbar icon now features a fully transparent background, resolving the previous white square problem, and the credits pill has been realigned to sit directly under the window controls on Windows. Key changes include the introduction of a new script for generating brand icons and adjustments to the layout for better visual consistency across platforms.

items.title: Add Gradle Build Scripts for JetBrains Plugin in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the missing `build.gradle.kts` and `settings.gradle.kts` files for both the Face and Fish JetBrains plugins. These files were copied from a working sibling repository, ensuring that the Gradle builds for `FaceTransformMCP` and `FishMCP` will now succeed. Note that publishing to JetBrains Marketplace will require a separate manual upload due to permission issues.

items.title: Shorten Face MCP Description for Registry Compliance in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the `server.json` description for the `FaceTransformMCP` to comply with MCP Registry validation, reducing the length from 114 to 89 characters. The new description maintains the full list of capabilities while ensuring successful publishing to the MCP Registry. All other descriptions have been audited and confirmed to meet the required length.

items.title: Enhance README Ownership Comments in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the required ownership comment to the top of 6 MCP README files, ensuring compliance with the MCP Registry's validation process. The changes affect the `aichat`, `face`, `fish`, `glm`, `grok`, and `webextrator` directories, allowing successful publishing without ownership validation errors. Each comment aligns with the corresponding PyPI package name, streamlining the publishing workflow.

items.title: New WorkBuddy Integration Tutorial in Backend 🚀
items.summary: We've added a comprehensive tutorial for integrating WorkBuddy with Ace Data Cloud, located in the new documentation file. This guide covers all UI field names and advanced configuration options, ensuring users can easily connect various OpenAI-compatible endpoints. The tutorial includes verification of model IDs and provides essential screenshots for clarity.

items.title: Enhance NSIS Installer with Branding and Path Safety in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant improvements to the NSIS installer for Windows, addressing critical issues related to installation paths and user interface branding. The installer now automatically appends 'AceData' to the installation directory if the user attempts to install to a bare drive or folder root, preventing potential data loss. Additionally, the installer features a customized UI with branded icons, headers, and sidebars, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Fix Windows Icon Transparency and Computer Use Runtime in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses two key issues in the Windows desktop client: the taskbar icon now displays with a transparent background, and the Computer Use functionality is restored by ensuring native modules are properly loaded. Key changes include a new script for generating the Windows icon and modifications to the build process to include necessary rebuild steps for native dependencies.

items.title: Enhance Desktop Experience with Draggable Chrome and Credits Pill Fix in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves key usability issues in the Nexior desktop application, specifically for Windows and macOS. A new draggable top bar has been added to allow users to move the window easily, while the credits pill has been repositioned to avoid overlap with native window controls. These enhancements improve the overall user experience and accessibility of the application.

items.title: Enhance Windows Experience for Nexior in Index 🎉
items.summary: This update addresses six user-reported usability issues with the Nexior desktop application on Windows. Key improvements include adjustments to the window drag functionality, icon transparency, and enhancements to the NSIS installer. These changes aim to provide a smoother user experience and resolve critical visual and functional bugs.

items.title: Fix notarization configuration for macOS in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a configuration issue with the macOS notarization process in electron-builder 26.x. The `mac.notarize` setting has been changed from an object to a boolean, ensuring compliance with the new schema requirements. This fix allows for successful packaging and notarization without further changes to the CI environment.

items.title: Enhance image handling by prioritizing inline data in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where the system was incorrectly prioritizing unreachable URLs over available inline base64 data when processing image edits. The code now ensures that inline base64 data is preferred for mirroring, significantly improving reliability. Verification steps confirm that the changes function as intended, enhancing the overall image handling process.

items.title: Enhance Group Rename Functionality in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue with the group rename feature that failed when accessed via the background API server. By forcing the foreground state before opening the Chat Info, the inline editing now works seamlessly. Validation on the production server confirms that the rename function operates correctly and persists the new group name.

items.title: Enhance Group Management with Rename API in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new API endpoint, **`POST /api/groups/rename`**, allowing users to rename existing WeChat groups seamlessly. The implementation ensures that the correct group is targeted by verifying the chat title before renaming, enhancing user experience and reducing errors. Additionally, the change includes comprehensive tests and validation to ensure reliability.

items.title: Fix image URL mirroring for GPT-Image-2 in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where the `gpt-image-2:official` model returned dead image URLs from an unreachable host. By enforcing a `b64_json` response format for the official relay, images are now correctly mirrored to our CDN, ensuring reliable access. Key changes include modifications to the routing and client handling to support this new format without affecting other models.

items.title: Enhance Local MCP Server Configuration in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a user-friendly UI for configuring local MCP servers directly within Nexior, eliminating the need for manual edits. Users can now add, edit, or remove MCP server configurations, with changes taking effect immediately without restarting the app. Additionally, 15 new localization keys have been added to support multiple languages.

items.title: Update history drawer to include GitHub Copilot in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the Coding Bridge history drawer by including GitHub Copilot in the introductory message across all 18 locales. The change ensures that users are aware of Copilot sessions alongside Claude Code and Codex sessions. No component or logic changes were made, as the necessary icons and functionality were already in place.

items.title: Enhance Android Computer Use with Permission Entry and Consent Dialog in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two significant features for the Android Computer Use functionality. Users can now pre-authorize the Accessibility service directly in Settings, along with an on-demand consent dialog that replaces the previous confirmation method. These enhancements provide users with better control over permissions and actions, improving the overall experience on Android devices.

items.title: Enhance Visual Grounding with Set-of-Mark in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the Set-of-Mark (SoM) visual grounding technique to the Android Computer Use feature. It enables the model to interact with tappable elements by displaying a numbered box on each element, allowing for precise actions based on visual cues. Key improvements include the addition of `observe()` and `tapMark(n)` methods, enhancing the user experience and reliability of interactions.

items.title: Enhance Copilot History Support in Coding Bridge 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new reader for GitHub Copilot transcripts in Nexior's Coding Bridge, allowing users to load Copilot sessions alongside existing Claude Code and Codex sessions. Key changes include modifications to `history.py` to integrate Copilot session handling and the addition of regression tests to ensure functionality. The implementation has been verified against real local transcripts, ensuring accurate session listing and event parsing.

items.title: Enhance Quote Activation for API Server in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue where the API server could not activate the Quote menu due to Windows foreground restrictions. The fix involves a new method that forces the foreground window, allowing for successful quote replies. Validation tests confirm that the new implementation consistently activates the correct Quote element without relying on potentially risky keyboard navigation.

items.title: Update max_turns default for scheduled tasks in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update increases the default value for max_turns in scheduled tasks from 30 to 50, aligning it with the backend changes in AceDataCloud/PlatformService. The input range remains unchanged at 1-50, ensuring that users can still configure their tasks as needed. All relevant locales have been updated to reflect this change.

items.title: Boost Scheduled Task Turn Budget to 50 in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update increases the default `DEFAULT_SCHEDULED_MAX_TURNS` for unattended scheduled tasks from 30 to 50, aligning it with the interactive chat default. The change ensures that unattended agents have the same turn budget, preventing issues with truncated multi-step processes. Other configurations remain unchanged, maintaining user control over task limits.

items.title: Update TypeScript ESLint Parser to Latest Version in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the @typescript-eslint/parser from version 8.62.0 to 8.62.1, aligning it with other projects. The new version includes fixes for various ESLint plugins, enhancing the overall linting experience. No code changes were made, ensuring a smooth transition.

items.title: Enhance Scheduled Tasks with Max Turns Control in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new 'Max turns' field in the scheduled task creation and editing forms, allowing users to set the maximum number of turns for agent tasks between 1 and 50, with a default of 30. This change addresses the previous silent cap of 10 turns, which often left multi-step tasks incomplete. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for various languages to ensure a seamless user experience.

items.title: Enhance Scheduled Tasks with Configurable Max Turns in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update raises the default `max_turns` for scheduled aichat2 tasks from 10 to 30, allowing for more extensive task execution without silent truncation. Users can now configure this value up to a maximum of 50, improving the flexibility of multi-step Bash and skill agent runs. Additionally, safeguards have been implemented to preserve existing task configurations during updates.

items.title: Enhance Engineering Skills with Grilling Techniques in Index 🔥
items.summary: This update introduces two new engineering skills, `grill-me` and `grilling`, to improve alignment between users and coding agents. The `grill-me` skill initiates a pre-build interview, while `grilling` serves as the reusable interview loop. Additionally, grilling is now enabled by default for non-trivial work, ensuring better understanding before coding begins.

items.title: New iOS Computer Use Design Document in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive technical design document for iOS computer use, detailing two feasible routes due to sandbox limitations. The document outlines the capabilities, architecture, and phased rollout strategy, while also providing a capability matrix and evidence from various sources. Key changes include the addition of a new iOS plan and modifications to existing Android documentation.

items.title: Enhance live tool progress streaming in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the `progress` field to the SSE operator whitelist, allowing the live rendering of tool-call arguments during long executions. Previously, the running block would appear empty while the model processed commands, leading to a poor user experience. A vitest regression test has been included to ensure the `progress` field is correctly handled in future updates.

items.title: Enhance Android Computer Use with Semantic Targeting and Foreground Session in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces semantic node-tree targeting to improve the accuracy of UI interactions, allowing the model to tap elements based on their labels rather than pixel coordinates. Additionally, a new foreground session service ensures that Nexior remains responsive while backgrounded, featuring an authoritative kill switch for user control. These enhancements significantly boost the reliability and user experience of the Android Computer Use feature.

items.title: Enhance Quote Activation via PostMessage in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the Quote menu activation issue when using the API server, which previously failed to send quoted replies. The solution involves using PostMessage for clicking the Quote item, ensuring it works independently of the window's foreground status. Tests have been updated accordingly, and all relevant checks have passed.

items.title: Add quote-reply feature for messages in Wisdom ✨
items.summary: This update introduces a quote-reply capability for text messages, allowing users to reply to specific on-screen messages in WeChat. The new `quote_text` field enhances context in busy group chats by enabling replies that reference previous messages. Additionally, robust validation and fallback mechanisms ensure reliable functionality even when the target message is not visible.
